q.zh: 沃尔夫冈·莫扎特出生在哪里？

options.zh: 英国伦敦

options.en: Vienna, Austria according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 奥地利维也纳（按书中另一种说法）
法国巴黎
奥地利萨尔茨堡

explain.en: The book says Leopold Mozart walked into a chapel in Salzburg, Austria, with his two small children, Wolfgang and Nannerl.
explain.zh: 书中写道，利奥波德·莫扎特带着两个年幼的孩子沃尔夫冈和南内尔走进了奥地利萨尔茨堡的一座小教堂。

q.en: How old was Wolfgang when he and his sister Nannerl began their concert tour?
q.zh: 沃尔夫冈和姐姐南内尔开始巡回演出时，他几岁？

options.zh: 6岁
8岁
4岁
5岁

explain.en: The book states: 'The following year, when Wolfgang was only six years old, he and Nannerl started on tour.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：“第二年，沃尔夫冈只有6岁时，他和南内尔就开始了巡回演出。”

q.en: In the story, the word 'prodigy' is used to describe young Wolfgang. What does 'prodigy' mean?
q.zh: 在故事中，“神童（prodigy）”一词用来形容小时候的沃尔夫冈。它的意思是什么？

options.zh: 一位音乐老师

options.en: A lazy child who avoids practice according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一个逃避练习的懒惰孩子（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A child who is very skilled at something, often more than most adults
options.zh: 在某个方面非常擅长的孩子，往往超过大多数成年人
一种乐器

explain.en: The glossary defines 'prodigy' as 'a child who is very skilled at something, often more skilled than most adults.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将“prodigy”定义为“在某个方面非常擅长的孩子，往往比大多数成年人还熟练”。

q.en: At the monastery, why did the monks think the organ was playing by itself?
q.zh: 在修道院里，僧侣们为什么以为管风琴自己在演奏？

options.zh: 因为南内尔在躲藏

options.en: Because Wolfgang was so small they could not see him
options.zh: 因为沃尔夫冈太小了，他们看不见他
因为没有风

options.en: Because the organ was broken according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 因为管风琴坏了（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says Wolfgang was so small the monks could not see him, so 'they thought the organ was playing itself' and cried, 'It's an angel!'
explain.zh: 书中说沃尔夫冈太小，僧侣们看不见他，于是“以为管风琴自己在演奏”，并惊呼：“是天使！”

q.en: As a poor adult, why did Wolfgang and his wife Constance dance around their apartment?
q.zh: 成年后生活贫困时，沃尔夫冈和妻子康斯坦茨为什么在公寓里跳舞？

options.en: Because they were teaching their children
options.zh: 因为他们在教孩子

options.en: Because they were practicing for a show
options.zh: 因为他们在为演出排练

options.en: To try to keep warm because they could not afford fuel
options.zh: 为了试着保暖，因为他们买不起取暖的燃料

options.en: Because they were celebrating a new job according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 因为他们在庆祝找到新工作（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says they were 'dancing to try and keep warm, because they could not afford fuel.'
explain.zh: 书中说他们“跳舞是为了保暖，因为他们买不起燃料”。

q.en: Why does the author include the story of Wolfgang meeting Princess Marie Antoinette?
q.zh: 作者为什么要写沃尔夫冈遇见玛丽·安托瓦内特公主的故事？

options.en: To explain how he learned to read according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了说明他是怎样学会阅读的（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To show that Wolfgang was charming and friendly as a child
options.zh: 为了表现沃尔夫冈小时候就很有魅力、待人友好
为了描写一个可怕的时刻
为了表现他不喜欢王室

explain.en: The book says Wolfgang 'was also very charming' and that people were 'smitten with this little musician,' which is why the Marie Antoinette story is told.
explain.zh: 书中说沃尔夫冈“也很有魅力”，人们都“被这个小小音乐家迷住了”，这正是讲述玛丽·安托瓦内特故事的原因。

q.en: Who actually ordered Mozart to write the Requiem?
q.zh: 究竟是谁请莫扎特创作《安魂曲》的？

options.en: The emperor of Austria according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 奥地利皇帝（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A count, who wanted to play it at his wife's funeral
options.zh: 一位伯爵，他想在妻子的葬礼上演奏它
他的妻子康斯坦茨
莫扎特本人

explain.en: The book reveals: 'The truth was that the requiem had been ordered by a count. He wanted to play the music at his wife's funeral.'
explain.zh: 书中揭示：“事实是，《安魂曲》是一位伯爵订制的。他想在妻子的葬礼上演奏这首曲子。”

q.en: In the book, what is a 'requiem'?

options.en: A royal title according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一个王室头衔（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）
一种钢琴
一种舞蹈

options.en: A piece of music written specifically for a funeral or death
options.zh: 专门为葬礼或逝者创作的音乐作品

explain.en: The glossary defines 'requiem' as 'a piece of classical music written specifically for a funeral or death.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将“requiem”定义为“专门为葬礼或逝者创作的古典音乐作品”。

q.en: What main lesson does the book's ending about the monument in Vienna suggest?
q.zh: 书中关于维也纳纪念碑的结尾暗示了什么主要信息？

options.en: Mozart is still considered the greatest musician who ever lived
options.zh: 莫扎特仍被认为是有史以来最伟大的音乐家
莫扎特被永远遗忘了

options.en: Mozart died rich and famous according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 莫扎特富有而知名地离世（按书中另一种说法）
莫扎特只写了一首歌

explain.en: The book states: 'Mozart is still considered the greatest musician who ever lived,' and notes a monument in Vienna marks the probable site of his grave.
explain.zh: 书中写道：“莫扎特仍被认为是有史以来最伟大的音乐家”，并提到维也纳有一座纪念碑标示他可能的安葬地。

q.en: What does the title 'Eine Kleine Nachtmusik' mean in German?
q.zh: 《Eine Kleine Nachtmusik》这个标题在德语中是什么意思？

options.zh: 小夜曲（一点夜晚的音乐）
一支王室舞蹈

options.en: A big loud song according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一首又大又吵的歌（按书中另一种说法）
一段晨祷

explain.en: The book explains that 'Eine Kleine Nachtmusik' means 'a little night music' in German.
explain.zh: 书中解释说，《Eine Kleine Nachtmusik》在德语中是“一点夜晚的音乐”（小夜曲）的意思。

prompt.en: If you could meet young Wolfgang Mozart, what question would you ask him about his music, and why?
prompt.zh: 如果你能见到小时候的沃尔夫冈·莫扎特，你会问他一个关于音乐的什么问题？为什么？

en: Think about what was unusual about Wolfgang's childhood and talents.
Use details from the book, such as hearing a piece once and memorizing it, to shape your question.
zh: 想想沃尔夫冈的童年和才华有什么不寻常之处。
运用书中的细节（比如听一遍曲子就能记住）来构思你的问题。
